Contagion (2011)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $75,658,097Details
International Box Office $61,545,842
Worldwide Box Office $137,203,939
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $14,640,837
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $6,258,653
Total $20,899,490
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Contagion follows the rapid progress of a lethal airborne virus that kills within days. As the fast-moving epidemic grows, the worldwide medical community races to find a cure and control the panic that spreads faster than the virus itself. At the same time, ordinary people struggle to survive in a society coming apart.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$22,403,596 (29.6% of total gross)
Legs:3.38 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:55.1% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$60,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.3 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,222 opening theaters/3,222 max. theaters, 5.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $114,107,298

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: September 9th, 2011 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
September 9th, 2011 (IMAX) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: November 10th, 2011 (Wide) (New Zealand)
November 26th, 2020 (Limited) (Australia)
Video Release: January 3rd, 2012 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for disturbing content and some language.
(Rating bulletin 2182, 7/27/2011)
Running Time: 105 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Ensemble
Film format: IMAX: DMR
Plot point: Death of a Spouse or Fiancée / Fiancé, Doctors, Internet, Medical and Hospitals, News, Non-Chronological, Young Child Dealing with the Death of a Parent
Social setting: End of the World, Epidemic
Subgenre: Survival Thriller
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Thriller/Suspense
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Warner Bros., Participant Media, Image Nation Abu Dhabi, Double Feature Films
Production Countries: United States, United Arab Emirates
Languages: English

Synopsis

Contagion follows the rapid progress of a lethal airborne virus that kills within days. As the fast-moving epidemic grows, the worldwide medical community races to find a cure and control the panic that spreads faster than the virus itself. At the same time, ordinary people struggle to survive in a society coming apart.

Opening Weekend:$22,403,596 (29.6% of total gross)
Legs:3.38 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:55.1% (domestic box office/worldwide)

Theater counts:3,222 opening theaters/3,222 max. theaters, 5.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $114,107,298
